group_runs.title: Tuesday Night Intervals
group_runs.address: Ferry Field, Palmer Park, Argo Loop, B2B Trail, the Arb + more

group_runs.title: Saturday Morning Group Run
group_runs.distance: longer workouts on marked course · beginner + fast groups

events.title: A2SF Top of the Park — Swing Dance Lessons & Djangophonique
events.venue: Top of the Park (Ingalls Mall & Washington St)
events.description: Swing dance lessons at 5:30 PM followed by Djangophonique (Gypsy jazz) at 8:15 PM. Final week of A2SF's Top of the Park, running through June 28. Outdoor, all ages.

events.title: A2 Dance Revolution — Salsa Mondays
events.description: Weekly salsa classes at 6:30 PM (Level 1 + 3 simultaneously, then Level 2 + 4 at 8 PM). Instructors Laura and Luis. No partner or experience needed. Runs every Monday.

events.title: Ian Flanigan — The Ark
events.description: Country/Americana singer with 'worn leather' vocals — Voice finalist with a road-tested sound. One of The Ark's periodic free donation shows.

events.title: A2 Dance Revolution — Bachata Wednesdays
events.description: Weekly bachata classes at 6:30 PM with social dancing after. Level 1+3 from 6:30–7:45 PM, Level 2+4 from 8–9:15 PM. Runs every Wednesday.

events.title: Esther Rose — The Ark
events.description: Country/pop artist on her fifth album, blending pop instincts with country storytelling. Intimate venue, great sightlines.

events.title: A2SF Top of the Park — Jive Colossus
events.venue: Top of the Park (Ingalls Mall & Washington St)
events.description: High-energy swing/jazz band. Free outdoor concert at A2SF's nightly Top of the Park series.

events.title: Handmade Moments — The Ark
events.description: New Orleans duo playing American roots music with diverse instrumentation and tag-team beatboxing. Expect surprises.

events.title: Gregory Stovetop — The Ark
events.description: Cosmic folk/rock/soul from Chelsea, Michigan-based singer-songwriter — wry lyrics, warm sound, the kind of artist who fills intimate rooms for a reason.

events.title: The RFD Boys — The Ark
events.description: Legends of Michigan bluegrass, performing together since 1969. A local institution at a local institution.

events.title: A2SF Top of the Park — My Brightest Diamond
events.venue: Top of the Park (Ingalls Mall & Washington St)
events.description: Shara Nova's indie art-pop project — a genuinely singular voice blending chamber pop and theatrical performance. Final weekend of A2SF Top of the Park.

events.title: Matt Watroba — The Ark
events.description: Celebrating 40 years in radio and 50 years performing American roots music — a Michigan folk/roots legend.

events.title: A2SF Top of the Park — Lady Sunshine and the X Band
events.venue: Top of the Park (Ingalls Mall & Washington St)
events.description: Energetic funk/soul/rock band. Closing night of A2SF Top of the Park 2026.

events.title: The Ark's Open Stage
events.description: Open mic — performers get eight minutes or two songs each. Followed by outdoor film screening at Top of the Park.

events.title: Holly Near with Jan Martinelli — The Ark
events.description: Peace-focused folk singer integrating spiritual and theatrical elements across a 50-year career. One of the most distinctive voices in American folk.

events.title: Blake Whyte — The Ark
events.description: Soul/theatre songwriter-actor — theatrical storytelling through music, strong voice.

events.title: Pixie and the Partygrass Boys — The Ark
events.description: High-energy Utah bluegrass band known for raucous, fun performances. Pre-4th-of-July energy.

events.title: The Ark's Open Stage
events.description: Open mic — eight minutes or two songs per performer.

events.title: Davy Knowles — The Ark
events.description: Isle of Man-born blues-rock guitarist with serious chops and an unmistakable style across acoustic, mandolin, and electric guitar.

events.title: UMMA Gallery Visit — 'Zina Saro-Wiwa: Table Manners'
events.description: Digital displays exploring identity and cultural practice by British-Nigerian artist Zina Saro-Wiwa. Also on view: 'American Sampler: Activating the Archive' and 'We Write To You About Africa.' Free admission to all UMMA galleries.

events.title: Ann Arbor Art Fair
events.description: One of the nation's largest juried art fairs — nearly 1,000 artists across 30 city blocks. Three simultaneously running fairs: Street Art Fair, Summer Art Fair, State Street Art Fair. Thu Jul 16 10am–9pm, Fri Jul 17 10am–9pm. Last two days within your stay.
